title: Amethyst Nether
description: Amethyst Nether is a datapack and mod that transforms the Nether with new content.
body:
Amethyst Nether is a datapack and mod that transforms the Nether by giving it a new appearance, adding biomes, structures, mobs, items, recipes, advancements, and more. ## 📝 Description The project was released in December 2022. It was created by amethyst137 and continued by bbschef, who is currently in charge. It is recommended to use Amethyst Nether in new worlds, and it **cannot be removed once applied without a reset** (reading the wiki is recommended to learn how to apply it correctly). World generation uses custom noise values and density functions to modify the terrain, giving it a more cavernous appearance. It includes rivers, caves, canyons, and more. ## ✨ Features
Biomes New biomes are added, and vanilla biomes are modified. For more information, read below.
Vanilla #### Basalt Deltas Biome with negative temperature. It is usually found near warped-type biomes. The terrain contains big basalt columns, lava pools, gravel patches, and other features. This gives the biome a dusty appearance. Magma cubes are very common (70%), while ghasts are uncommon (30%). Locate with `/locate biome minecraft:basalt_deltas`. #### Crimson Forest Biome with positive temperature. It is usually found near soul-type biomes. The terrain contains big crimson fungi, weeping vines, fallen fungi, and crimson vegetation. This gives the biome an overgrown appearance. Hoglins are very common (70%), while piglins are uncommon (30%). Locate with `/locate biome minecraft:crimson_forest`. #### Nether Wastes Biome with neutral temperature and humidity. It can be found around all Nether biomes. The terrain contains netherrack hills, lava pools, gold and quartz rocks, and other features. This gives the biome a barren appearance. Zombified piglins are very common (70%), ghasts are uncommon (20%), and magma cubes are rare (10%). Locate with `/locate biome minecraft:nether_wastes`. #### Soul Sand Valley Biome with negative humidity. It is usually found near crimson-type biomes. The terrain contains big basalt pillars, basalt rocks, cracked nether fossils, and skeleton skulls. This gives the biome a desolate appearance. Skeletons are very common (60%), while ghasts are common (40%). Locate with `/locate biome minecraft:soul_sand_valley`. #### Warped Forest Biome with positive humidity. It is usually found near basalt-type biomes. The terrain contains big warped fungi, twisting vines, fallen fungi, and warped vegetation. This gives the biome an eerie appearance. Endermen are very common (100%). Locate with `/locate biome minecraft:warped_forest`.
Modded #### Black Peaks Biome with negative temperature and humidity. It is usually found near basalt- and soul-type biomes. The terrain contains big blackstone spikes, amethyst geodes, and amethyst clusters. This gives the biome a jagged appearance. Skeletons are very common (70%), while ghasts are uncommon (30%). Locate with `/locate biome an:black_peaks`. #### Crimson Plains Biome with positive temperature. It is usually found along the edges of crimson-type biomes. The terrain contains lava lakes, gold rocks, crimson vegetation, and other features. This gives the biome a sparse appearance. Hoglins are very common (80%), while piglins are uncommon (20%). Locate with `/locate biome an:crimson_plains`. #### Basalt Flows Biome with generally negative temperature and humidity. It is usually found below basalt-dominated biomes. The terrain contains big lava flows, floating basalt formations, and other features. This gives the biome a volcanic appearance. Striders are common (100%). Locate with `/locate biome an:basalt_flows`. #### Nether Flows Biome with generally positive temperature and humidity. It is usually found below netherrack-dominated biomes. The terrain contains big lava flows, floating netherrack formations, and other features. This gives the biome a volcanic appearance. Striders are common (100%). Locate with `/locate biome an:nether_flows`. #### Old Growth Crimson Forest Biome with positive temperature. It is usually found in the center of crimson-type biomes. The terrain contains huge 2×2 crimson fungi, weeping vines, fallen fungi, leaf litter, and other features. This gives the biome an ancient appearance. Hoglins are very common (70%), while piglins are uncommon (30%). Locate with `/locate biome an:old_growth_crimson_forest`. #### Old Growth Warped Forest Biome with positive humidity. It is usually found in the center of warped-type biomes. The terrain contains huge 2×2 warped fungi, twisting vines, fallen fungi, leaf litter, and other features. This gives the biome an ancient appearance. Endermen are very common (100%). Locate with `/locate biome an:old_growth_warped_forest`. #### Sulfur Fields Biome with negative temperature and humidity. It is usually found near basalt- and soul-type biomes. The terrain contains sulfur springs, lava pools, sulfur spikes, and other features. This gives the biome a toxic appearance. Sulfur cubes are very common (100%). A gas mask is required for safe exploration. Locate with `/locate biome an:sulfur_fields`. #### Warped Plains Biome with positive humidity. It is usually found along the edges of warped-type biomes. The terrain contains lava lakes, quartz rocks, warped vegetation, and other features. This gives the biome a sparse appearance. Endermen are very common (100%). Locate with `/locate biome an:warped_plains`. #### Wart Heap Biome with positive temperature and negative humidity. It is usually found near crimson- and soul-type biomes. The terrain contains big heap fungi, nether wart patches, red and brown mushrooms, and other features. This gives the biome a fungal appearance. Zombified piglins are very common (70%), ghasts are uncommon (20%), and skeletons are rare (10%). A gas mask is required for safe exploration. Locate with `/locate biome an:wart_heap`.
Structures New structures and structure templates are added. For more information, read below.
Structures #### Bastion Castle Grand Bastion variant (1/3). It is usually found in crimson-type biomes. The structure contains multiple towers, large bridges, and a central castle with several rooms. This gives the structure an imposing appearance. It includes chests and barrels with (5) loot tables. Piglin swordsmen, crossbowmen, and axemen can be found around the structure. Locate with `/locate structure an:bastion_castle`. #### Bastion Gallery Grand Bastion variant (2/3). It is usually found in crimson-type biomes. The structure contains two towers, a central platform, and a lava pool below. This gives the structure an elegant appearance. It includes chests and barrels with (3) loot tables. Piglin swordsmen, crossbowmen, and axemen can be found around the structure. Locate with `/locate structure an:bastion_gallery`. #### Bastion Prison Grand Bastion variant (3/3). It is usually found in crimson-type biomes. The structure contains two towers and multiple prison cells holding sporebacks. This gives the structure a dungeon-like appearance. It includes chests with (3) loot tables. Piglin swordsmen, crossbowmen, and axemen can be found around the structure. Locate with `/locate structure an:bastion_prison`. #### Red Fortress Nether fortress-style structure. It is usually found in Nether Wastes. The structure contains large bridges, long halls, and small rooms. This gives the structure a fortified appearance. It includes chests with (1) loot table. Wither skeleton swordsmen can be found around the structure. Locate with `/locate structure an:red_fortress`.
Structure templates #### Basalt Vent Smoke Vent variant (1/2). It is usually found in Basalt Deltas. It consists of natural basalt formations that release smoke into the environment. This gives the structure a natural appearance. Place with `/place feature an:5_surface_structure/formation/basalt_vent`. #### Blackstone Vent Smoke Vent variant (2/2). It is usually found in Black Peaks. It consists of natural blackstone formations that release smoke into the environment. This gives the structure a natural appearance. Place with `/place feature an:5_surface_structure/formation/blackstone_vent`. #### Soul Tomb Decorative structure. It is usually found in Soul Sand Valley. It consists of small polished blackstone tombs. This gives the structure a somber appearance. It may contain barrels with (1) loot table. Place with `/place feature an:8_underground_decoration/ambient/soul_tomb`. #### Warped Ruin Decorative structure. It is usually found in warped-type biomes. It consists of small polished blackstone ruins covered with warped wart. This gives the structure an abandoned appearance. It includes chests with (1) loot table. Some variants may contain traps. Place with `/place feature an:8_underground_decoration/ambient/warped_ruin`.
Mobs New variants are added. For more information, read below.
Biome variants #### Ghosted Skeleton variant exclusive to Soul Sand Valley with a 15% spawn chance. It wears a chainmail chestplate and wields an enchanted sword. There is a 25% chance for it to spawn with a bow. All of its attacks set the player on fire. There is a 2.5% chance for it to spawn as a jockey variant. It includes (1) death loot table. Summonable with `/loot give @s loot an:uncraftable/eggs/ghosted_spawn_egg`. #### Sporeback Zombified piglin variant exclusive to Wart Heap with a 25% spawn chance. It wears leather pants and may spawn with or without a weapon. It is covered with fungi and releases spores. It attacks both players and other piglins. It includes (1) death loot table. Summonable with `/loot give @s loot an:uncraftable/eggs/sporeback_spawn_egg`. #### Volatile Sulfur Cube Sulfur cube variant exclusive to Sulfur Fields with a 35% spawn chance. It is slightly larger than a normal sulfur cube. If it detects a non-sneaking player within 4 blocks, it causes a large explosion. It includes (1) death loot table. Summonable with `/loot give @s loot an:uncraftable/eggs/volatile_sulfur_cube_spawn_egg`.
Structure variants #### Piglin (Grand Bastion) Piglin variant exclusive to Grand Bastions. It consists of (4) variants: axeman, crossbowman, rider, and swordsman. There is a 25% chance for a variant to spawn with a piece of netherite equipment. There is a 15% chance for the rider variant to spawn in crimson-type biomes. It includes (4) death loot tables. Summonable with `/loot give @s loot an:uncraftable/eggs/piglin_swordsman_spawn_egg`. #### Wither Skeleton (Red Fortress) Wither skeleton variant exclusive to Red Fortress. It consists of (1) variant: swordsman. There is a 25% chance for it to spawn with a piece of netherite equipment. It includes (1) death loot table. Summonable with `/loot give @s loot an:uncraftable/eggs/wither_skeleton_swordsman_spawn_egg`.
Items New functional items are added. For more information, read below.
Banners #### Bridge Banner Uncraftable banner exclusive to Red Fortress. It is red and features a blaze design. Obtainable with `/loot give @s loot an:uncraftable/functional/bridge_banner`. #### Greed Banner Uncraftable banner exclusive to Grand Bastions. It is yellow and features a piglin snout design. Obtainable with `/loot give @s loot an:uncraftable/functional/greed_banner`.
Heads #### Ghosted Skull Skull obtained by killing a Ghosted, with a 5% drop chance. It has a unique sound when placed on a note block. Obtainable with `/loot give @s loot an:uncraftable/functional/ghosted_skull`. #### Piglin Brute Head Head obtained by killing a Piglin Axeman in Grand Bastions, with a 5% drop chance. It has a unique sound when placed on a note block. Obtainable with `/loot give @s loot an:uncraftable/functional/piglin_brute_head`. #### Sporeback Head Head obtained by killing a Sporeback, with a 5% drop chance. It has a unique sound when placed on a note block. Obtainable with `/loot give @s loot an:uncraftable/functional/sporeback_head`.
Recipes New craftable items are added. For more information, read below.
Equipment #### Gilded Netherite Helmet Armor piece craftable with 1 netherite upgrade smithing template, 1 netherite helmet, and 1 gold block in a smithing table. Grants +4 armor, +3 armor toughness, +15% knockback resistance, and reduces burning time by 20%. Durability: 468. It can be repaired in an anvil using a gold ingot. Obtainable with `/loot give @s loot an:craftable/equipment/gilded_netherite_helmet`. #### Gilded Netherite Chestplate Armor piece craftable with 1 netherite upgrade smithing template, 1 netherite chestplate, and 1 gold block in a smithing table. Grants +9 armor, +4.5 armor toughness, +25% knockback resistance, and reduces burning time by 40%. Durability: 681. It can be repaired in an anvil using a gold ingot. Obtainable with `/loot give @s loot an:craftable/equipment/gilded_netherite_chestplate`. #### Gilded Netherite Leggings Armor piece craftable with 1 netherite upgrade smithing template, 1 netherite leggings, and 1 gold block in a smithing table. Grants +7 armor, +4 armor toughness, +20% knockback resistance, and reduces burning time by 30%. Durability: 638. It can be repaired in an anvil using a gold ingot. Obtainable with `/loot give @s loot an:craftable/equipment/gilded_netherite_leggings`. #### Gilded Netherite Boots Armor piece craftable with 1 netherite upgrade smithing template, 1 netherite boots, and 1 gold block in a smithing table. Grants +4 armor, +2.5 armor toughness, +10% knockback resistance, and reduces burning time by 10%. Durability: 553. It can be repaired in an anvil using a gold ingot. Obtainable with `/loot give @s loot an:craftable/equipment/gilded_netherite_boots`. #### Gilded Netherite Shield Armor piece craftable with 1 netherite upgrade smithing template, 1 shield, and 1 gold block in a smithing table. Grants +10% knockback resistance and +60% explosion knockback resistance. Fire-resistant when dropped. Durability: 420. It can be repaired in an anvil using a gold ingot. Obtainable with `/loot give @s loot an:craftable/equipment/gilded_netherite_shield`.
Food #### Crimson Soup Food item craftable with 1 red mushroom, 1 crimson fungus, 1 nether wart, and 1 bowl. It restores +6 hunger and +1.2 saturation. When consumed, there is a 30% chance to grant fire resistance for 30 seconds. It is stackable. Obtainable with `/loot give @s loot an:craftable/food/crimson_soup`. #### Enchanted Golden Carrot Food item craftable with 8 gold blocks and 1 carrot. It restores the same hunger and saturation as a normal golden carrot. When consumed, it grants invisibility for 2:30 minutes, jump boost II for 30 seconds, night vision for 2:30 minutes, and speed I for 1:30 minutes. Obtainable with `/loot give @s loot an:craftable/food/enchanted_golden_carrot`. #### Warped Stew Food item craftable with 1 brown mushroom, 1 warped fungus, 1 porkchop, and 1 bowl. It restores +8 hunger and +1.6 saturation. When consumed, there is a 30% chance to receive nausea for 30 seconds. It is stackable. Obtainable with `/loot give @s loot an:craftable/food/warped_stew`.
Misc #### Bomb Item craftable with 1 string, 2 gunpowder, 1 fire charge, and 1 iron ingot. It is a throwable explosive used with right-click. It is recommended to use it with caution. Obtainable with `/loot give @s loot an:craftable/misc/bomb`. #### Crimson Funglet Item craftable with 8 bone meal and 1 crimson fungus. When placed on nylium, it grows a crimson fungus. Obtainable with `/loot give @s loot an:craftable/misc/crimson_funglet`. #### Gas Mask Item craftable with 3 leather, 2 tinted glass, 1 coal block, and 3 netherite scrap. It is required to enter toxic biomes. It grants +2 armor and has 64 durability. While used in toxic biomes, it loses 1 durability every minute. It can be repaired in an anvil using coal. Obtainable with `/loot give @s loot an:craftable/misc/gas_mask`. #### Heap Funglet Item craftable with 8 bone meal and 1 nether wart. When placed on soul soil, it grows a heap fungus. Obtainable with `/loot give @s loot an:craftable/misc/heap_funglet`. #### Warped Funglet Item craftable with 8 bone meal and 1 warped fungus. When placed on nylium, it grows a warped fungus. Obtainable with `/loot give @s loot an:craftable/misc/warped_funglet`.
Mechanics #### Impassable roof Starting at Y=156, ender pearls break when they hit bedrock. This is designed to prevent players from passing through the bedrock roof. #### Piglin zombification When a piglin or hoglin enters the Wart Heap biome, it transforms into an undead after a short time. It can become a Sporeback and attack both players and other piglins. #### Toxic biomes Biomes such as Sulfur Fields and Wart Heap are toxic for both the player and other entities. A gas mask is required to avoid negative effects such as poison or weakness.
Ore distribution New ore distributions are added. For more information, read below.
Generic #### Ancient Debris Ore can be found throughout the Nether from Y=0 to Y=160. It is recommended to mine in flow-type biomes at Y≈26 using fire resistance potions, or in cave-type biomes at Y≈140 using explosives. #### Glowstone Ore can be found throughout the Nether from Y=80 to Y=160. It is recommended to mine in any Nether biome at Y≈105, or in Sulfur Fields, where it has a ×1.67 generation multiplier. A gas mask is required in Sulfur Fields.
Basalt biomes #### Deepslate Coal Ore Ore can be found throughout the Nether from Y=0 to Y=160. It is recommended to mine at ground level in Basalt Deltas, or in Basalt Caves at Y≈140 using a pickaxe. #### Deepslate Gold Ore Ore can be found throughout the Nether from Y=0 to Y=160. It is recommended to mine at ground level in Basalt Deltas, or in Basalt Caves at Y≈140 using a pickaxe. #### Deepslate Iron Ore Ore can be found throughout the Nether from Y=0 to Y=160. It is recommended to mine at ground level in Basalt Deltas, or in Basalt Caves at Y≈140 using a pickaxe.
Netherrack biomes #### Nether Gold Ore Ore can be found throughout the Nether from Y=0 to Y=160. It is recommended to mine at ground level in crimson-type biomes, where it has a ×1.67 generation multiplier, or in Nether Caves at Y≈140 using a pickaxe. #### Nether Quartz Ore Ore can be found throughout the Nether from Y=0 to Y=160. It is recommended to mine at ground level in warped-type biomes, where it has a ×1.67 generation multiplier, or in Nether Caves at Y≈140 by mining quartz geodes using a pickaxe.
Technical details Technical information is provided. For more details, read below.
Adjust mob spawn rates 1. Use the datapack version of the project. 2. Open the ZIP file. 3. Navigate to the biome file you want to adjust: - `data → an → worldgen → biome` (for a custom biome). - `data → minecraft → worldgen → biome` (for a vanilla biome). 4. Open the selected biome file. 5. Locate the `"spawners"` section. 6. Adjust the mob weight values as you like and save the changes. Note: It is recommended to keep the total at 100. For example, in Soul Sand Valley, you can lower the skeleton weight from 75 to 65 and raise the ghast weight from 25 to 35.
Remove a specific custom entity 1. Use the datapack version of the project. 2. Open the ZIP file. 3. Navigate to `data → an → function → entities` and open `sort_entity.mcfunction`. 4. Locate the section for the entity you want to remove. 5. Delete the entire line and save the changes. The selected custom entity will no longer be generated.
Remove a specific custom item 1. Use the datapack version of the project. 2. Open the ZIP file. 3. Navigate to `data → an → recipe → (select the category)` and delete the item recipe. 4. Navigate to `data → an → advancement → recipe → (select the category)` and delete the item recipe advancement. 5. Save the changes. If the item is also added through a loot table: 1. Complete all previous steps first. 2. Navigate to `data → an → loot_table → chests → grand_bastion` and open the loot table file. 3. Find `"type": "minecraft:loot_table"` and replace its value with `"an:uncraftable/empty"`. 4. Save the changes.
Remove all custom entities 1. Use the datapack version of the project. 2. Open the ZIP file. 3. Navigate to `data → an → function → timer` and open `1s.mcfunction`. 4. Locate the `## entities sorting` section. 5. Delete the entire line and save the changes. Custom entities will no longer be generated.
Remove impassable roof 1. Use the datapack version of the project. 2. Open the ZIP file. 3. Navigate to `data → an → function → timer` and open `tick.mcfunction`. 4. Locate the `### ender_pearl`. 5. Delete the entire line and save the changes. Ender pearls will now behave as in vanilla.
Reset 1. Leave the Nether and exit the world. 2. Make a backup of your world. 3. Install [NBTExplorer](https://github.com/jaquadro/NBTExplorer). 4. Open the backup using NBTExplorer. 5. Open `/datapacks` and delete the datapack. 6. Delete the `/DIM-1` folder. 7. Open `/level.dat`. 8. In `Data → DataPacks → Enabled`, delete Amethyst Nether. 9. In `Data → WorldGenSettings → dimensions`, delete `minecraft:the_nether`. 10. Save the changes. The Nether is now fully reset, and you can install a newer version of Amethyst Nether or any other datapack or mod.
## 📌 Notes - Not compatible with datapacks or mods that change Nether world generation, such as Incendium, Amplified Nether, BetterNether, or Biomes O' Plenty. - Compatible with datapacks or mods that add Nether structures, such as Dungeon and Taverns or Uncovery. - Still in development; new content will be added in future versions. For updates, join the [Discord](https://discord.gg/rkzQXctkXj).
